named_technique: Interval-filling completeness (Brown's criterion) on merged power-atoms with harmonic-mass gap bound S(X) ‚Č• (m‚ąí1)¬∑‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1)
seminal_paper_arxiv: Brown 1961, Amer. Math. Monthly 68, 557-560 (interval-filling completeness criterion); ErdŇĎs 1962, Acta Arith. 7 (complete sequences). [NOTE: the 'Cassels 1960' attribution for the interval-filling lemma is a PHANTOM ‚ÄĒ correct refs are Brown 1961 + ErdŇĎs 1962.]
bridge_lemma: For the merged atom multiset A = {d^j : d‚ąąD, j‚Č•0}, at every threshold X the partial sum S(X) of atoms ‚Č§ X equals ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} (d¬∑x_d ‚ąí 1)/(d‚ąí1) where x_d is the largest power of d that is ‚Č§ X, and the next atom is m = min_{d‚ąąD}(d¬∑x_d). Since d¬∑x_d ‚Č• m for every d, S(X) ‚Č• (m‚ąí1)¬∑‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• m‚ąí1 under the hypothesis ‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• 1, so the interval-filling gap condition (Brown 1961) m ‚Č§ S(X)+1 holds for all X. With the seed 1 = d^0 ‚ąą B_d, the subset-sums of A then fill [0, S(X)] for every X, giving ‚ąĎ_D B_d = ‚Ąē.
informal_proof_outline:
Theorem (k=0). Let D be a finite set of integers all ‚Č• 3 with ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} 1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• 1. Then every nonnegative integer n is a sum ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} a_d with each a_d a sum of distinct powers of d (a_d ‚ąą B_d). Equivalently ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} B_d = ‚Ąē.

Step 1 (reduction to merged-atom subset-sums). An element of B_d is a sum of distinct powers d^j (j ‚Č• 0); summing over d, the pointwise sumset ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} B_d equals the set of subset-sums of the merged multiset A = {d^j : d ‚ąą D, j ‚Č• 0}, each atom used once. The value 1 = d^0 occurs once per base, so its multiplicity is |D| and is load-bearing (e.g. 2 = 1+1 needs two bases). Verified bit-for-bit: merged subset-sum = ‚ąĎ_D B_d, zero differences on [0,300000] for {3,4,7},{3,4,5},{3,4}.

Step 2 (interval-filling lemma ‚ÄĒ Brown's criterion, Amer. Math. Monthly 68 (1961); NOT Cassels). Sort A ascending as a_1 ‚Č§ a_2 ‚Č§ ‚Ä¶ with partial sums S_i. If a_1 = 1 and a_{i+1} ‚Č§ S_i + 1 for every i, then the subset-sums of {a_1,‚Ä¶,a_i} cover [0, S_i] for every i, hence cover all of ‚Ąē. (Induction: adjoining a_{i+1} ‚Č§ S_i + 1 extends [0,S_i] to [0,S_i+a_{i+1}] since the translate overlaps or abuts.) Here a_1 = 1 = d^0 ‚ąą A.

Step 3 (the gap condition holds for all X ‚ÄĒ the key estimate). Fix X ‚Č• 1. For each base d let x_d = d^{J_d} be the largest power of d with d^{J_d} ‚Č§ X (exists since d^0 = 1 ‚Č§ X). The atoms ‚Č§ X from base d are d^0,‚Ä¶,d^{J_d}, summing to the geometric series (d¬∑x_d ‚ąí 1)/(d‚ąí1). Hence S(X) = ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} (d¬∑x_d ‚ąí 1)/(d‚ąí1). The next atom is m = min_{d‚ąąD}(d¬∑x_d) (each d¬∑x_d = d^{J_d+1} is the smallest power of d exceeding X). Since d¬∑x_d ‚Č• m for every d,
  S(X) ‚Č• ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} (m ‚ąí 1)/(d‚ąí1) = (m ‚ąí 1)¬∑‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} 1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• m ‚ąí 1,
the last step using ‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• 1. So m ‚Č§ S(X) + 1; as X was arbitrary, the gap condition holds at every step.

Step 4 (conclusion). By Steps 2‚Äď3 the subset-sums of A cover [0, S(X)] for every X, and S(X) ‚Üí ‚ąě, so they are all of ‚Ąē; by Step 1, ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} B_d = ‚Ąē. ‚ąé

Sharpness. The inequality S(X) ‚Č• (m‚ąí1)¬∑‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) shows ‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• 1 is exactly the threshold: for ‚ąĎ < 1 the gap condition first fails at a finite atom, leaving a permanent gap (e.g. {3,4}, ‚ąĎ=5/6: fails at atom 64, prior sum 61, so 62 is non-representable ‚ÄĒ verified), matching Pomerance's converse.
candidate_lemmas: merged_atom_identity: ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD} B_d = { subset-sums of the multiset {d^j : d‚ąąD, j‚Č•0} }, each atom used at most once (value 1 = d^0 occurs with multiplicity |D|).
geometric_partial_sum: the atoms ‚Č§ X from base d sum to (d¬∑x_d ‚ąí 1)/(d‚ąí1), where x_d = largest power of d that is ‚Č§ X.
next_atom_min: the smallest atom exceeding X is m = min_{d‚ąąD}(d¬∑x_d), and d¬∑x_d ‚Č• m for every d.
harmonic_lower_bound: S(X) := ‚ąĎ atoms ‚Č§ X = ‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD}(d¬∑x_d‚ąí1)/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• (m‚ąí1)¬∑‚ąĎ_{d‚ąąD}1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• m‚ąí1 when ‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) ‚Č• 1.
brown_interval_filling (Brown 1961, NOT Cassels): if a_1 = 1 and a_{i+1} ‚Č§ S_i + 1 for all i, the subset-sums of the sorted atom sequence cover [0, S_i] for all i, hence cover ‚Ąē.
seed_one: 1 = d^0 ‚ąą B_d for every d, so the merged atom sequence has smallest atom 1, seeding the interval-filling induction.
honest_disclaimer: WITHDRAWN ‚ÄĒ KNOWN, DO NOT SUBMIT (team-lead gate verdict + scholar prior-art): Alexeev 2025 public Lean proof Erdos124b.lean (plby/lean-proofs, 407 lines, Dec 2025; Bloom/Xena blog) + corollary of Brown 1961 (Amer. Math. Monthly 68, 557-560) + ErdŇĎs 1962 (Acta Arith. 7); the 'Cassels 1960' attribution is a PHANTOM. Retained ONLY as an independent re-derivation / machinery calibration; never to be fired; no successor should resurrect it as a submission. This is a COMPLETE, CORRECT, but KNOWN elementary proof (no transcendence, no gcd hypothesis at k=0) ‚ÄĒ fit_score 0.9 reflects high confidence it is provable/verifiable, NOT novelty (it is a textbook corollary of Brown 1961, see framing below). The only Mathlib-level work is: a finite Finset geometric-series identity, the min/inequality manipulation, and a Nat induction for the interval-filling lemma; all are elementary and within reach. The inequality S(X) ‚Č• (m‚ąí1)¬∑‚ąĎ1/(d‚ąí1) was verified exactly (rational arithmetic) with 0 violations on 8000 random X up to 10^15 across D = {3,4,7},{3,4,5},{3,5,7,13},{3,4,6}; the merged-atom = sumset identity and contiguity-from-0 were verified vs a ground-truth subset-sum engine. Caveat for the verifier: the value 1 appears once per base in the merged multiset ‚ÄĒ the multiplicity is load-bearing (2 = 1+1 needs two bases) and must be preserved; treating A as a set rather than a multiset would be incorrect for the small atoms. FRAMING (verified against the BEGL96 PDF + scholar's literature check): this is the s=0 ANALOGUE, OUTSIDE the BEGL96 conjecture ‚ÄĒ BEGL define Pow(A;s) with s ‚Č• 1 (verbatim), so s=0 (with d^0=1 as an atom) is not part of their statement. The CONJECTURE is ErdŇĎs [Er97]; resolved by Alexeev 2025 with a PUBLIC Lean proof (Erdos124b.lean, plby/lean-proofs, 407 lines) that reproves Brown's criterion constructively. This statement is KNOWN, NOT NOVEL ‚ÄĒ it is the textbook corollary of Brown's interval-filling completeness criterion (Amer. Math. Monthly 68, 1961; with ErdŇĎs 1962, Acta Arith. 7) ‚ÄĒ the 'Cassels 1960' attribution is a phantom; Thomas Bloom (erdosproblems.com) characterized exactly this proof as 'the obvious inductive way that generalises powers of 2 ... a single inequality.' This dossier is a clean Lean ADJUDICATION TARGET for a known result, NOT a novelty claim. The result proved here is the contiguous-from-0 form (‚ąĎ_D B_d = ‚Ąē exactly), STRICTLY STRONGER than erdos124.zero's eventual form (‚ąÄŠ∂† n in atTop) ‚ÄĒ the seed 1 = d^0 is what gives 'from 0' rather than 'eventually'. The s ‚Č• 1 case (BEGL's actual conjecture) is NOT covered: this proof's gap-condition loses the 1-seed at s ‚Č• 1 (smallest atom becomes d_min^s ‚Č• 3) and the harmonic mass degrades to ‚ąĎ d^{1‚ąís}/(d‚ąí1) < 1, which is exactly where the difficulty (Mignotte‚ÄďWaldschmidt spacing) enters.
